notes: Verified via WebSearch 2026-07-11 (Kuwait PAM manpower.gov.kw, akhbrna.ae, Gulf News, Arab Times): daily outdoor work ban 11:00-16:00 from June 1 to August 31 each year, legal basis is the permanent Private Sector Labor Law No. 6 of 2010, re-issued annually via an executive ministerial resolution (deliberately did NOT cite this year's specific resolution number/year to keep content evergreen).
Exemptions: technical necessity / non-deferrable emergency work, with prior approval from the Labor Inspection Department and full compliance with occupational safety requirements.
Employer obligations in all exempted cases: cold drinking water, first aid, cooling means, shaded rest areas — sourced directly from official PAM decision summary.

Deliberately avoided citing a specific fine amount (secondary sources mentioned an unconfirmed ~$325/worker figure) — used general 'legal penalties under Law No. 6/2010, possible suspension of employer file' phrasing instead to avoid an unverifiable, possibly-outdated number.
Confirmed no contamination with Saudi Arabia's separate midday work ban (June 15 - Sept 15) which appeared in mixed search results — all facts here are Kuwait-specific (June 1 - Aug 31).
Single-country event (_countryCode: kw), aliasSlugs: [] to avoid base_country_leakage.

core.type is 'fixed' anchored to June 1 (start date, the dominant search intent); the August 31 end date is covered thoroughly in FAQ, aboutEvent, and schemaData.endDate instead, since the engine's fixed type only supports one countdown anchor.
